---
title: "RUITONG LI"

---

::: {data-aos="fade-up"}
# 🧑‍🚀 Space Crew: RUITONG LI

**Faculty:** \[ICDI\] *(Notice: Student ID strictly hidden)*
:::

------------------------------------------------------------------------

::::::: columns
::: {.column width="48%" data-aos="fade-right"}
### 📸 Solo at NARIT (1pt)
![](images/clipboard-110598780.png){.rounded}
:::

::: {.column width="4%"}
:::

:::: {.column width="48%" data-aos="fade-left"}
### 🎥 Personal Vlog (1pt)

::: {.ratio .ratio-16x9 style="border-radius: 20px; overflow: hidden;"}
<iframe
  src="https://www.youtube.com/embed/2SYnYexTMmQ"
  title="NARIT Field Trip Video"
  frameborder="0"
  allow="accelerometer; autoplay; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; gyroscope; picture-in-picture; web-share"
  allowfullscreen>
</iframe>
:::
::::
:::::::

------------------------------------------------------------------------

::: {data-aos="fade-up"}
### 💭 Personal Impressions (1pt)

Visiting NARIT left a deep impression on me. Its spacious area and
modern architecture made the entire visit both relaxing and enjoyable,
allowing me to learn and experience things at a leisurely pace. The
planetarium also showed me how science and technology help humanity
understand the universe.

### 💡 Knowledge Gained (1pt)

From this visit, I learned some basic knowledge about astronomy. I also
learned that science centers can use space design and simple technology
to help people understand science more easily.

### 🚀 Future Applications (1pt)

This area is suitable for different groups of people. It has elevators
and wheelchair friendly paths for elderly visitors and people with
disabilities, many seats for resting, and slides for children to play.
In the future, this experience can help me think about how public spaces
should be designed for everyone.
:::
